title: fp-ts + Effect dual library strategy
decision: Use fp-ts (pipe, TaskEither, ReaderTaskEither) for all business logic pipelines. Use Effect/Schema only for HTTP schema definitions (@effect/schema). Do not mix in same module.
rationale: fp-ts was the original choice; Effect was introduced for schema validation. Mixing both fully in pipelines creates confusion. Clear boundary: Effect for schema, fp-ts for logic.

title: OpenAI structured output: all fields required
decision: All Effect/Schema objects used in OpenAI structured output must have every property required. Use Schema.NullOr() instead of Schema.optional().
rationale: OpenAI structured output rejects schemas with optional fields. Schema.optional() maps to optional JSON schema which causes API errors.

title: OptionFromNullishToNull returns effect/Option not fp-ts/Option
decision: Use O.getOrNull() from effect/Option, never O.toNullable() from fp-ts/Option, when unwrapping OptionFromNullishToNull values.
rationale: OptionFromNullishToNull returns effect/Option. Calling fp-ts O.toNullable() on it causes TypeORM to serialize the Option wrapper object instead of the raw value, silently corrupting data.
